examples.title: Calculate Carbon Footprint for IT Operations
examples.description: Algorithm to estimate annual CO2 emissions from computer systems based on energy consumption
examples.code:
ALGORITHM CalculateCarbonFootprint
INPUT: powerConsumption (Watts), hoursPerDay, daysPerYear, carbonIntensity (kg CO2/kWh)
OUTPUT: annualCarbonKg

BEGIN
 // Convert power from Watts to Kilowatts
 powerKW = powerConsumption / 1000
 
 // Calculate annual energy consumption in kWh
 annualHours = hoursPerDay * daysPerYear
 annualEnergyKWh = powerKW * annualHours
 
 // Calculate carbon footprint
 annualCarbonKg = annualEnergyKWh * carbonIntensity
 
 PRINT "Annual Energy Consumption: " + annualEnergyKWh + " kWh"
 PRINT "Annual Carbon Footprint: " + annualCarbonKg + " kg CO2"
 
 // Suggest optimization if above threshold
 IF annualCarbonKg > 1000 THEN
 PRINT "Consider: virtualization, power management, energy-efficient hardware"
 END IF
 
 RETURN annualCarbonKg
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm calculates the carbon footprint of IT equipment by considering power consumption, usage patterns, and grid carbon intensity. It helps organizations quantify their environmental impact and identify optimization opportunities.

examples.title: E-waste Recycling Decision Tree
examples.description: Algorithm to determine the best disposal method for electronic waste
examples.code:
ALGORITHM EwasteRecycleDecision
INPUT: deviceType, condition, componentValue
OUTPUT: recommendedAction

BEGIN
 PRINT "Evaluating E-waste disposal option..."
 
 // Check if device can be reused
 IF condition == "functional" THEN
 IF componentValue > 500 THEN
 recommendedAction = "Refurbish and resell"
 ELSE
 recommendedAction = "Donate for reuse"
 END IF
 // Check if components can be recovered
 ELSE IF condition == "repairable" THEN
 recommendedAction = "Harvest components for repairs"
 // Check recyclable value
 ELSE IF condition == "non-functional" THEN
 IF componentValue > 100 THEN
 recommendedAction = "Deep recycling for precious metals"
 ELSE
 recommendedAction = "Standard material recycling"
 END IF
 // Hazardous disposal required
 ELSE
 recommendedAction = "Certified hazardous waste disposal"
 END IF
 
 PRINT "Recommended Action: " + recommendedAction
 RETURN recommendedAction
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm helps determine the most environmentally responsible way to dispose of electronic waste based on the device's condition and value. It prioritizes reuse over recycling and ensures proper hazardous disposal when necessary.

examples.title: Calculate Data Center PUE
examples.description: Algorithm to calculate Power Usage Effectiveness and evaluate efficiency
examples.code:
ALGORITHM CalculatePUE
INPUT: totalFacilityPower, itEquipmentPower
OUTPUT: pueValue, efficiencyRating

BEGIN
 // Calculate PUE
 IF itEquipmentPower > 0 THEN
 pueValue = totalFacilityPower / itEquipmentPower
 ELSE
 PRINT "Error: IT power cannot be zero"
 RETURN
 END IF
 
 // Determine efficiency rating
 IF pueValue <= 1.2 THEN
 efficiencyRating = "Excellent - World Class"
 ELSE IF pueValue <= 1.5 THEN
 efficiencyRating = "Good"
 ELSE IF pueValue <= 2.0 THEN
 efficiencyRating = "Average"
 ELSE
 efficiencyRating = "Poor - Needs Improvement"
 END IF
 
 PRINT "PUE: " + pueValue
 PRINT "Rating: " + efficiencyRating
 
 // Calculate energy waste percentage
 wastePercentage = ((pueValue - 1) / pueValue) * 100
 PRINT "Energy used for non-IT: " + wastePercentage + "%"
 
 RETURN pueValue
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm calculates PUE to measure data center energy efficiency. A lower PUE indicates less energy is wasted on cooling, lighting, and other non-computing needs, contributing to greener IT operations.
